front: Film: Jack Torrance pursues his family with an axe in the Overlook Hotel, famously saying 'Here's Johnny!'; ends with Jack frozen in a hedge maze; the axe scene drew on Victor Sjostrom's 1921 Swedish silent film The Phantom Carriage
Film: floor-level tracking shots follow Danny on his blue-seated tricycle; he sees a torrent of blood from an elevator; the ghost twin girls were inspired by a Diane Arbus photograph

Film: 'Midnight, the Stars and You' plays in the Gold Room as a slow zoom reveals Jack anachronistically in a photo of a 1921 party at the Overlook Hotel; bathroom scene has red walls, white ceiling/floor — a man mentions 'correcting' his daughters while wiping Jack's jacket

Film: main theme by Wendy Carlos and Rachel Elkind adapts the 'Dream of a Witches' Sabbath' from Berlioz's Symphonie Fantastique; Penderecki's Utrejna, Polymorphia, Awakening of Jacob, and two De Natura Sonoris pieces are used; Ligeti's Lontano also appears
Film: a Penderecki piece plays when Danny on his tricycle turns the corner to see the twins; Bartok's 'night music' plays during a crane shot between a model and what it represents

Film: directed by Stanley Kubrick; adapted from a Stephen King novel inspired by the Stanley Hotel in Colorado; in the novel, chef Dick Halloran rushes to Colorado but is attacked by topiaries; Shelley Duvall played Wendy and denied being professionally abused by Kubrick
